meta.title: Example: Turning AI Into a Repeatable Workflow

guide.question: How can a team make AI output more stable across repeated work?
guide.summary: The article argues that AI adoption becomes useful only when prompts, checks, tools, and review loops are turned into a repeatable operating system.
chain: evaluation and feedback loops improve reliability
workflow design matters more than one-off prompting

blocks.title: The Problem Is Unstable Delivery
blocks.source_markdown: The author starts from a common team problem: AI can produce impressive one-off answers, but repeated delivery is still uneven. The gap is not only model capability; it is the lack of shared standards for context, tools, review, and acceptance.

analysis.本块一句话: The opening defines the real problem as unstable repeated delivery, not whether the model can produce one impressive answer.
analysis.主论点: AI becomes operationally valuable only when teams can make repeated outputs stable.

子论点.论点: One-off output quality is a weak adoption metric.
子论点.依据: The author contrasts impressive demos with uneven repeated delivery.
子论点.解释: A team cannot rely on AI operationally if quality depends on luck or individual prompting habits.

analysis.事实核查 / 可信度: This is primarily an operational claim, not a factual claim. It is credible but should be validated against the team's own delivery data.
analysis.信息缺口 / 反例: The article does not provide quantitative before/after failure rates. For exploratory tasks, one-off creativity may still be enough.

blocks.title: Workflow Standards Raise the Floor
blocks.source_markdown: The author then proposes converting repeated tasks into documented workflows: required context, tool calls, review gates, examples, and feedback loops. The point is not to limit AI, but to make baseline delivery reliable.

analysis.本块一句话: The method is to turn repeated work into a documented workflow with inputs, checks, examples, and review gates.
analysis.主论点: Reusable workflow standards raise the minimum quality of AI-assisted work.

子论点.论点: Review gates prevent silent quality drift.
子论点.依据: The author lists review gates as a required part of the workflow.
子论点.解释: Without acceptance checks, failures may look fluent and pass unnoticed.

analysis.事实核查 / 可信度: No external factual claim requires verification in this block.
analysis.信息缺口 / 反例: The article does not define how to measure whether a gate is effective. Overly rigid workflows may slow down novel work.

insights.洞察: The useful unit of AI adoption is not a prompt, but a repeatable workflow with review gates.
insights.对应原文块: The Problem Is Unstable Delivery; Workflow Standards Raise the Floor
insights.为什么对我重要: This shifts the implementation question from writing better prompts to designing reusable delivery systems.
insights.可能反例或边界: For early exploration, rigid workflow design can be premature.

items.建议: Pick one repeated task and define its input checklist, tool steps, acceptance gate, and feedback loop.
items.对应洞察: The useful unit of AI adoption is a repeatable workflow.
items.后续动作: Run the workflow on three real cases and record where quality fails.
